base_case: Barker v. Lull Engineering — SYNTHETIC COUNTERFACTUAL VARIANT (not a real decided case)
constructor: Venkateshwar Reddy Jambula (drafted with Claude Opus as programming and writing assistant)

perturbations.original_text: The gravity of injury from the loader's operation under the facts of this case was minor — bruising and a sprained ankle, no rollover, no ejection, no catastrophic injury.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (gravity of loss) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f1 (gravity_of_loss). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The probability of injury under foreseeable use was demonstrably low — the loader was extensively pre-production tested under the specific terrain conditions at issue and performed safely in those tests.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (probability of harm) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f2 (probability_of_harm). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: Whether a safer alternative design was mechanically feasible in this case is treated as an unresolved question on the record — both sides offered competing engineering evidence and neither clearly prevailed. The court does not develop this factor as load-bearing.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (alternative precautions) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f3 (alternative_precautions). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The cost of any attempted safer alternative design would have increased the loader's manufacturing cost by more than 40%, making it unaffordable for the contractors who specifically needed this loader for their intended commercial function. The burden is substantial.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (burden of precaution) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f4 (burden_of_precaution). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The loader's intended utility — tight-radius operation on construction sites — would have been substantially degraded by any attempted safer design. The manufacturer specifically evaluated alternatives and rejected them to preserve the product's intended commercial function.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (product utility) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f5 (product_utility). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

reconstruction_check_prompt: Does the case description above omit any factor you believe a court would typically weigh in this analysis? If yes, name the omitted factor and explain what weight you would assign it. If no, say so.
